summary: Read-only multi-agent audit of the SerenityUI SwarmUI/ComfyUI-clone graph runtime: IR, executor, dispatch, JSON codec, workflow execution, safety — vs ComfyUI

reviews.summary: The IR is not a node/edge struct graph: it is a fixed-point worklist over ~30 parallel `List[...]` "typed value tables" (value_nodes/value_ports/value_types plus per-type side tables for model/cond/image/mask/latent/noise/sampler/sigmas/scalar). JSON→IR is three importers (Comfy API prompt, Comfy UI canvas, Ideogram4 export) that all canonicalize into a typed nodes/edges shape and then run a single executor `apply_typed_workflow_graph` (workflow_graph.mojo:1602-2909). Validation is genuinely fail-loud: a strict node allowlist (1621-1696), per-edge from/to checks, duplicate-node-id and duplicate-SetNode-name detection, typed-input requirement checks, and a no-progress trap (2902) that rejects cycles. The HTTP layer caps bodies at 1 MiB (serenity_daemon.mojo:90,3539) and maps the `[501] ` sentinel to HTTP 501 (3255-3257), so malformed/oversized/unknown-node graphs do not silently degrade to txt2img — that boundary is solid. The real problems are: a stale divergent SECOND copy of the entire executor living dead inside serenity_daemon.mojo (675-965) that no longer matches the shipping one; quadratic-to-cubic parsing because every input link is re-resolved by full linear edge scans on every worklist pass; substring-based type acceptance that can silently mis-route handles; and several typed handles (GetImageSize INT, GetNode-carried scalars) whose value-table entry has no backing scalar, turning graphs ComfyUI runs fine into 501s. No recursion (loop is iterative) and the 1 MiB cap bounds OOM, so crash/hang risk is low; the dominant risks are silent mis-execution and parse-cost blowup.

kill_vectors.title: Dead, divergent duplicate executor inside the daemon will rot and mislead/regress
kill_vectors.location: serenitymojo/serve/serenity_daemon.mojo:675-965 (_apply_workflow_graph_ir, _apply_workflow_params, _looks_like_ideogram4_comfy_ui_export, _apply_ideogram4_comfy_ui_export)
kill_vectors.mechanism: parse_generate (serenity_daemon.mojo:1213) calls the IMPORTED workflow_graph.apply_workflow_params, not the file-local _apply_workflow_params. I grepped every caller: _apply_workflow_params/_apply_workflow_graph_ir have ZERO callers (only the internal chain at 871-912). This is a full second copy of the graph IR + executor that is months behind the real one (it only handles CheckpointLoaderSimple/CLIPTextEncode/EmptyLatentImage/KSampler/ModelSamplingAuraFlow, infers polarity from node TITLE at 932 which the real executor explicitly stopped doing, and lacks the entire typed value-table executor). It silently mis-execute risk: any future maintainer who edits/extends the daemon-local copy thinking it is live ships nothing, or worse a reviewer reads it and concludes the daemon infers prompt polarity from titles.
kill_vectors.evidence: grep showed `_apply_workflow_params`/`_apply_workflow_graph_ir` defined at 862/675 with no call sites; parse_generate imports `apply_workflow_params as apply_workflow_graph_params` (serenity_daemon.mojo:80) and calls that at 1213. The local copy at 929-937 still does `if lower_title.find("negative")>=0` polarity inference that workflow_graph.mojo abandoned.
kill_vectors.fix: Delete serenity_daemon.mojo:360-965 (the whole _looks_like_ideogram4/_apply_* duplicate block) and keep only the imported workflow_graph entrypoints. One executor, one IR, one allowlist.

kill_vectors.title: O(N^2 - N^3) graph parsing: links re-resolved by full edge scans every worklist pass
kill_vectors.location: serenitymojo/serve/workflow_graph.mojo:1781-2903 (while remaining>0 loop) + _workflow_find_input_link:553-567 + _workflow_value_index:635-641
kill_vectors.mechanism: The executor is a fixed-point loop: `while remaining>0: for each not-done node: resolve its inputs and check readiness`. _workflow_find_input_link does a full O(E) linear scan of edges; it is called once PER INPUT PER NODE PER PASS. A node not yet ready is revisited every pass, and there can be up to N passes (a deep chain resolves one node per pass). Each readiness check also calls _workflow_value_index which is an O(V) linear scan of the value table that only grows. Net worst case ~O(N^2 * E) plus O(N^2 * V). With the 1 MiB body cap a worst-case adversarial deep chain is a few thousand nodes, so this is CPU burn (hundreds of ms to seconds of pure parsing per request), not a hang — but it is on the synchronous request hot path and is re-paid on every /v1/generate even for identical graphs.
kill_vectors.evidence: Loop structure at 1781-1783; every node branch calls `_workflow_find_input_link(edges, node_id, ...)` (e.g. KSampler 2639-2648 issues 10 separate full edge scans) and `_workflow_value_index(value_nodes,...)` repeatedly; readiness failure just leaves done[i]=False so the node is rescanned next pass.
kill_vectors.fix: Build adjacency once: a Dict keyed by (to_node,to_port)->source link and a Dict keyed by (node_id,port)->value-table index, populated in a single pre-pass. Then Kahn topological order (indegree queue) instead of the rescan-until-stable loop. Turns parsing into O(N+E).

kill_vectors.title: Substring-based type acceptance can silently mis-route a handle into an incompatible slot
kill_vectors.location: serenitymojo/serve/workflow_graph.mojo:651-662 (_workflow_type_accepts), used by GetNode at 2076
kill_vectors.mechanism: _workflow_type_accepts treats a declared type as a comma list and does `wrapped.find(token)>=0` substring matching after wrapping in commas/spaces. The wrapping mitigates the worst prefix cases, but it is still a string-contains test rather than an exact token-set membership. Any declared type that legitimately contains an embedded comma-delimited token (custom multi-type GetNode outputs, future composite types) will accept a handle of a type the downstream node cannot actually consume. Because GetNode only uses this for the declared-vs-actual gate, a mis-accept lets e.g. a LATENT ride a slot a consumer treats as CONDITIONING, which then flattens into wrong genparams with no error — silent mis-execution, the exact failure the fail-loud design is trying to prevent.
kill_vectors.evidence: _workflow_type_accepts at 651-662 does three `.find()` substring probes; GetNode at 2070-2079 calls it with `declared = output_type` from the node fields, which on canvas import is copied verbatim from the JSON (comfy_ui_canvas_to_typed_graph:1276-1278).
kill_vectors.fix: Replace substring matching with explicit tokenize-on-comma then exact-equality membership; reject unknown tokens. Keep the `*`/empty/exact fast paths.

kill_vectors.title: GetImageSize / scalar typed handles have a value-table entry but no scalar backing -> valid Comfy graphs 501
kill_vectors.location: serenitymojo/serve/workflow_graph.mojo:2213-2223 (GetImageSize) vs scalar consumers e.g. _workflow_scalar_int:732 / _workflow_copy_value_metadata:915-918
kill_vectors.mechanism: GetImageSize emits width/height/batch_size as type INT via _workflow_add_value into value_nodes ONLY — it never appends to scalar_nodes/scalar_ints. Any consumer that reads those as numeric scalars (ImageScale.width link 2237, EmptyLatentImage.width 2917, or a SetNode carrying INT through the bus 915-918) calls _workflow_value_index(scalar_nodes,...) which returns -1 and raises '[501] scalar metadata missing source'. So a graph that ComfyUI runs trivially (GetImageSize -> ImageScale) fails loud in our importer. This is a correctness/contract gap: the value store advertises a typed INT handle that no scalar path can resolve, so the type system lies about what is consumable.
kill_vectors.evidence: GetImageSize at 2220-2222 adds INT to value table with no scalar append; _workflow_copy_value_metadata at 915-918 and _workflow_require_scalar_type at 719-721 both raise when scalar_nodes has no matching (node,port). The reboot doc itself flags this at WORKFLOW_EXECUTOR_REBOOT lines 143-144 ('GetImageSize still only emits typed INT handles ... it fails instead of inventing dimensions').
kill_vectors.fix: Either (a) do not emit INT value handles you cannot back with a scalar (emit nothing and fail at the consumer with a clear 'image dimensions unknown before render' message), or (b) record a sentinel scalar with an 'unresolved' flag so the consumer fails with a specific message rather than the generic 'scalar metadata missing source'. Make the value table and scalar table append atomically in one helper so they can never diverge.

kill_vectors.title: Parallel value-table side arrays can desync; a missing append corrupts later index reads
kill_vectors.location: serenitymojo/serve/workflow_graph.mojo:1731-1772 (table decls) + every node branch that appends to several Lists in sequence
kill_vectors.mechanism: The IR stores a value as: append to value_nodes/value_ports/value_types AND, in a separate hand-written statement, append the matching entry to the type-specific side table (model_names, cond_texts, latent_widths/heights/images/init/mask, etc.). These appends are not transactional. _workflow_copy_value_metadata (851-928) is 78 lines of manual, branch-specific appends; the NOISE/SAMPLER/SIGMAS branches (898-914) only append if an inner `for j ... break` finds the source — if it does not find it (e.g. the source row was itself a desynced/empty entry), the value-table row is added but the side-table row is NOT, permanently shifting every subsequent index lookup for that type by one. Index-based reads (latent_widths[latent_idx]) would then return a neighbor's data: silent value corruption (wrong width/seed/scheduler) with no crash.
kill_vectors.evidence: latent reads index by position e.g. 2678-2686 read latent_widths[latent_idx]/latent_heights[latent_idx]; _workflow_copy_value_metadata NOISE branch 898-901 appends only inside the matched `for` and has no else; SAMPLER 902-906 and SIGMAS 907-914 are the same pattern. value table and side tables are independent Lists with no length-coupling assertion.
kill_vectors.fix: Collapse the parallel arrays into one `List[WorkflowValue]` where WorkflowValue carries an enum-tagged payload (model_name / cond_text / latent dims / scalar union). One append = one row, impossible to desync. If staying with side arrays short-term, add `debug_assert(len(value_nodes)==len(value_types))` and make the NOISE/SAMPLER/SIGMAS branches raise on not-found instead of silently skipping the append.

kill_vectors.title: Whole-graph re-parse on every request; no IsChanged/output cache
kill_vectors.location: serenitymojo/serve/serenity_daemon.mojo:1210-1213 (parse_generate -> apply_workflow_graph_params) and workflow_graph.mojo:2912 apply_workflow_params
kill_vectors.mechanism: Every POST /v1/generate runs loads(body) then the full importer + executor from scratch. There is no caching of (a) the parsed JSON->typed-graph lowering or (b) per-node results keyed on input hash. A UI that nudges one widget (seed, steps) and resubmits the same 50-node graph re-pays the entire O(N^2) lowering. Low severity because it is correctness-neutral and bounded by the 1 MiB cap, but it is wasted latency on the interactive hot path.
kill_vectors.evidence: parse_generate at 1204-1213 unconditionally calls loads + apply_workflow_graph_params; no cache structure exists in workflow_graph.mojo (no Dict of prior results, no hash of the graph).
kill_vectors.fix: Cache the lowering keyed on a hash of the workflow sub-object; short-circuit to the cached flat genparams when only top-level scalar overrides changed. This is the cheap analog of ComfyUI's IsChangedCache.

comfy_comparison.how_comfy: ComfyUI's execution.py validates the whole prompt up front (validate_prompt) before any node runs, then builds an ExecutionList and walks it in topological order via a proper graph (nodes + typed input links), caching outputs in an IsChangedCache + HierarchicalCache/LRUCache keyed on a recursive hash of each node's resolved inputs, so an unchanged subgraph is skipped entirely. Each node executes in its own try/except that reports the FAILING node id + class_type + full traceback back to the UI, supports lazy inputs and node expansion/subgraphs, runs only the nodes feeding requested outputs (partial execution), and honors nodes.interrupt_processing for mid-run cancellation.
comfy_comparison.ours: We do strict pre-execution validation (allowlist 1621-1696, typed-input requirement checks, duplicate-id/name, no-progress cycle trap 2902) and fail loud with a 501 sentinel that the daemon maps to HTTP 501 (serenity_daemon.mojo:3255). But: execution is a rescan-until-stable worklist, not a real topo order; there is NO output/IsChanged caching (full re-parse per request); errors identify the node TYPE and sometimes (node_id:port) but there is no single structured 'failing node' envelope the UI can highlight (it is a flat string); there is no lazy evaluation, no partial-output execution (we lower the WHOLE graph to flat genparams regardless of which SaveImage is wired), and cancellation is a job-level concern, not node-level interrupt.
comfy_comparison.gap: Three concrete gaps vs Comfy: (1) no input-hash output cache -> we re-pay parsing the UI re-submits; (2) worklist instead of indexed topo sort -> O(N^2) where Comfy is O(N+E); (3) error reporting is a string, not a {node_id, class_type, reason} object, so the editor cannot light up the offending node the way Comfy's frontend does.
comfy_comparison.recommendation: Adopt three Comfy patterns, mapped onto our code: (a) before the executor, build a (to_node,to_port)->source and (node,port)->value-index Dict in one pass and run Kahn topo order — this is the ExecutionList analog and kills the O(N^2). (b) Add a lowering cache keyed on a hash of the workflow object (IsChangedCache analog) so repeat submits short-circuit. (c) Change every `raise Error('[501] ...')` in the per-node branches to carry the node id + type_id, and have the daemon serialize that into a JSON error body {node, type, reason} so SerenityUI can highlight the failing node exactly like Comfy's traceback-to-UI. Do NOT adopt Comfy's silent fallback behaviors — our fail-loud-on-unknown-node stance is correct and better than Comfy degrading.

coordination_gaps.title: Duplicated parsing logic: two full IR/executor copies
coordination_gaps.problem: workflow_graph.mojo owns the live executor; serenity_daemon.mojo:675-965 contains a stale second copy (_apply_workflow_graph_ir/_apply_workflow_params) that is never called and diverges in semantics (title-based polarity, tiny allowlist). Any contract change made in one place is invisible to a reader of the other.
coordination_gaps.fix: Delete the daemon-local copy; single-source the executor in workflow_graph.mojo.

coordination_gaps.title: Flat-key contract between IR and backends is a hand-maintained string list duplicated 3x
coordination_gaps.problem: apply_workflow_params copies a ~60-entry key allowlist for params (2948-2970) AND an identical one for genparams (2979-3001); the daemon's parse_generate then re-reads each field by name (1279-1289...) and ipc_codec must encode each. A new graph field (e.g. a new lanpaint_*) must be added in 4+ places by hand; miss one and the field is silently dropped between IR and backend with no error.
coordination_gaps.fix: Define the flattenable-field set once (a single comptime list or a shared struct) and iterate it in both the params and genparams branches and the JobParams reader.

coordination_gaps.title: Error format is an ad-hoc string sentinel, not a structured node-scoped error
coordination_gaps.problem: The IR raises plain `Error('[501] ...')`; the daemon string-matches the literal prefix `[501] ` (serenity_daemon.mojo:3255) and slices 6 chars off. Any branch that forgets the exact prefix/space becomes a 422 instead of 501, and the UI receives an opaque sentence with no machine-readable node id to highlight. The IR/daemon contract is 'a magic string prefix', which is fragile.
coordination_gaps.fix: Return a typed error (code + node_id + type_id + message) from the executor; let the daemon serialize it to JSON and the correct HTTP status. Stop string-prefix matching.

coordination_gaps.title: IR emits typed handles the downstream contract cannot resolve
coordination_gaps.problem: GetImageSize emits INT value handles with no scalar backing (2220-2222); the SetNode/GetNode bus advertises INT/FLOAT/STRING/BOOLEAN support (680) and Reroute/Switch copy 'scalar' metadata (915-928) that for these handles does not exist, so a structurally valid graph fails with a generic 'scalar metadata missing source'. The value-type contract and the scalar-resolution contract are not co-enforced.
coordination_gaps.fix: Make value-handle creation and scalar/side-table backing a single atomic operation so a handle of type T always has a resolvable T payload, or fail at emit with a specific message.

performance.title: Cubic-ish input resolution in the executor
performance.current: Every worklist pass calls _workflow_find_input_link (full O(E) edge scan, workflow_graph.mojo:553-567) once per input per not-done node, and _workflow_value_index (O(V) scan, 635-641) repeatedly; up to N passes. KSampler alone issues 10 edge scans (2639-2648).
performance.suggestion: Pre-build (to_node,to_port)->source-link and (node,port)->value-index Dicts in one pass, then Kahn topological order. O(N+E) instead of O(N^2*E).

performance.title: Full re-parse per request, no output cache
performance.current: parse_generate (serenity_daemon.mojo:1213) re-runs loads + the whole importer/executor for every /v1/generate, even when the UI changed one widget on the same graph.
performance.suggestion: Add an IsChanged-style cache keyed on a hash of the workflow object; short-circuit unchanged lowerings (Comfy IsChangedCache analog).

performance.title: Many redundant List.copy() of String per handle
performance.current: Branches append node_id/port/name and call .copy() on ports/types/texts/paths constantly (e.g. _workflow_add_scalar 707-712, copy_value_metadata throughout). Each handle carries several heap String copies.
performance.suggestion: Collapse parallel arrays into one List[WorkflowValue] struct (also fixes the desync kill-vector); intern repeated port/type strings to avoid per-row String allocations.

top_suggestions: DELETE the dead duplicate executor in serenity_daemon.mojo:360-965; it is never called, diverges from the live one, and is a regression/confusion trap. Single-source the IR+executor in workflow_graph.mojo.

Replace the rescan-until-stable worklist (workflow_graph.mojo:1781-2903) with a one-pass adjacency build (two Dicts) + Kahn topological sort. Kills the O(N^2 * E) parse cost and makes 'cyclic' vs 'missing input' diagnosable separately.

Collapse the ~30 parallel List side-arrays into a single List[WorkflowValue] with a tagged payload union. Eliminates the silent index-desync corruption class (kill-vector 5) and the GetImageSize/scalar contract lie (kill-vector 4), and cuts String copies.

Make errors structured: return {code, node_id, type_id, message} from the executor instead of the '[501] ' string sentinel that the daemon prefix-matches (serenity_daemon.mojo:3255). Lets SerenityUI highlight the failing node like ComfyUI's traceback-to-UI.
Replace substring-based _workflow_type_accepts (651-662) with exact comma-token membership to remove the silent mis-route risk on GetNode declared types.
Add an IsChanged-style lowering cache keyed on a hash of the workflow object so repeat submissions of the same graph (UI widget nudges) skip the full re-parse.

kill_vectors_verified.title: Dead, divergent duplicate executor inside the daemon will rot and mislead/regress
kill_vectors_verified.mechanism: parse_generate (serenity_daemon.mojo:1213) calls the IMPORTED workflow_graph.apply_workflow_params, not the file-local _apply_workflow_params. I grepped every caller: _apply_workflow_params/_apply_workflow_graph_ir have ZERO callers (only the internal chain at 871-912). This is a full second copy of the graph IR + executor that is months behind the real one (it only handles CheckpointLoaderSimple/CLIPTextEncode/EmptyLatentImage/KSampler/ModelSamplingAuraFlow, infers polarity from node TITLE at 932 which the real executor explicitly stopped doing, and lacks the entire typed value-table executor). It silently mis-execute risk: any future maintainer who edits/extends the daemon-local copy thinking it is live ships nothing, or worse a reviewer reads it and concludes the daemon infers prompt polarity from titles.
kill_vectors_verified.evidence: grep showed `_apply_workflow_params`/`_apply_workflow_graph_ir` defined at 862/675 with no call sites; parse_generate imports `apply_workflow_params as apply_workflow_graph_params` (serenity_daemon.mojo:80) and calls that at 1213. The local copy at 929-937 still does `if lower_title.find("negative")>=0` polarity inference that workflow_graph.mojo abandoned.
kill_vectors_verified.fix: Delete serenity_daemon.mojo:360-965 (the whole _looks_like_ideogram4/_apply_* duplicate block) and keep only the imported workflow_graph entrypoints. One executor, one IR, one allowlist.

verdict.evidence:
CONFIRMED — dead code with zero external callers:
grep of all .mojo/.py/.sh files confirms `_apply_workflow_params` (serenity_daemon.mojo:862) and `_apply_workflow_graph_ir` (serenity_daemon.mojo:675) have no callers outside the internal chain. The only external call site is:

  serenity_daemon.mojo:1213: `apply_workflow_graph_params(obj)`

which is the IMPORTED function from line 80:
  `from serenitymojo.serve.workflow_graph import apply_workflow_params as apply_workflow_graph_params`

The local `_apply_workflow_params` is called by nobody; `_apply_workflow_graph_ir` is called only by `_apply_workflow_params` (line 912), itself never called.

CONFIRMED — dead copy IS divergent from live executor:
The dead `_apply_workflow_params` params/genparams key lists (lines 885-903) are frozen at ~21 keys. The live `workflow_graph.apply_workflow_params` (lines 2946-3006) passes ~50 keys including `lanpaint_*`, `qwen_edit_conditioning_image`, `reference_image`, `inpaint_conditioning_*`, `outpaint_*`, `sample_caps_*`, `conditioning_mask_*`, `workflow_save_prefix` etc. The live executor also handles three additional graph formats the dead copy cannot: `looks_like_comfy_api_prompt_graph` (line 2923), `looks_like_comfy_ui_canvas_graph` (line 2926/2942), and the richer `apply_typed_workflow_graph` (line 3011) vs the dead copy's `_apply_workflow_graph_ir`.

FALSE — the specific claim that polarity-from-title was "explicitly abandoned" in the live executor:
The audit claims the dead copy's `lower_title.find("negative") >= 0` pattern (serenity_daemon.mojo:933) was something "the real executor explicitly stopped doing." This is wrong. The live `workflow_graph.apply_workflow_params` at lines 3031-3032 still contains the identical code:

  ```
  var lower_title = String(title.lower())
  if lower_title.find("negative") >= 0:
      _set_if_missing(obj, String("negative"), JSONValue.from_string(text))
  else:
      _set_if_missing(obj, String("prompt"), JSONValue.from_string(text))
  ```

This path is only reached for field-only graphs (nodes without edges), which is the legacy format. Both copies behave identically on that input. The live typed executor (`apply_typed_workflow_graph`) uses edge-graph topology to assign polarity and does not use title heuristics, but the field-only fallback in the live file still does. So the "dead copy infers polarity from titles while live one doesn't" claim is false for their shared fallback path.
verdict.claim: Dead, divergent duplicate executor inside the daemon will rot and mislead/regress
verdict.notes:
The dead-code diagnosis is correct: `_apply_workflow_params` and `_apply_workflow_graph_ir` have zero external callers and are never reached at runtime. The divergence claim is also correct: the dead copy is missing ~30 genparams keys and three entire graph-format handlers that the live executor in workflow_graph.mojo has grown.

However, the mechanism for harm is weaker than "high" implies. Because the functions are unreachable (not merely unguarded), the "silently mis-execute" scenario cannot happen today. The real risk is maintainer confusion: someone reading serenity_daemon.mojo sees a plausible-looking workflow executor and either edits it thinking changes will take effect, or draws wrong conclusions about system behavior (e.g., the title-polarity inference they see at line 933 also exists in the live code, but only in its field-only fallback).

The polarity-from-title claim is false for both copies: it exists in the live workflow_graph.mojo field-only path (lines 3031-3032) identically to the dead copy. This specific sub-claim inflated the severity.

Corrected severity: medium. The dead code should be deleted to eliminate confusion, but it poses no live regression or silent mis-execution risk.

reviews.section: Graph Executor / Lowering Engine (serenitymojo/serve/workflow_graph.mojo + serenity_daemon.mojo driver)
reviews.summary: The "executor" is not a tensor executor: apply_typed_workflow_graph (workflow_graph.mojo:1602-2909) is a pure, synchronous JSON-to-JSON metadata-lowering pass that walks a typed node/edge graph with a fixed-point worklist loop and flattens supported Comfy/Swarm t2i subgraphs into the flat JobParams contract. It is genuinely fail-loud: every unsupported node type, type mismatch, dangling link, duplicate id, cyclic/unresolved graph, and missing prompt raises an "[501]"-prefixed Error that serenity_daemon.mojo:3255 translates to HTTP 501, and all other parse errors become 422. That fail-loud discipline is the strongest part of this code and largely defeats the classic "unknown node silently degrades to txt2img" failure mode for the typed path. The real risks are elsewhere: a SECOND, legacy nodes-only adapter (workflow_graph.mojo:3014-3063) that DOES silently degrade by title-sniffing; an entire duplicated dead copy of the graph engine inside serenity_daemon.mojo (~lines 268-862) that has already diverged from the live one; an O(N^2)/worst-case O(N^3) worklist with parallel List[] "columns" instead of a value store; no output caching or partial execution so the whole graph re-lowers on every request and every widget tweak; and a couple of integer/cast edge cases. Because lowering runs at enqueue inside parse_generate and the executor holds no fds/handles/threads, there are no leak/deadlock/cancellation-race vectors inside the executor itself.

kill_vectors.title: Legacy nodes-only adapter silently degrades unknown graphs to title-sniffed txt2img
kill_vectors.location: workflow_graph.mojo:3014-3063 (reached from apply_workflow_params via wf.contains("nodes") with NO "edges" array)
kill_vectors.mechanism: When a workflow body has `nodes` but no `edges` array (and is not detected as ideogram4/comfy-api/canvas/params/genparams), control falls into a second, untyped adapter. It loops nodes and for CLIPTextEncode decides positive-vs-negative purely by `title.lower().find("negative")` (3031-3035), copies KSampler/EmptyLatent fields with NO link validation, and treats VAEDecode/SaveImage as no-ops. Any node type not in its tiny if/elif DOES raise 501 (3057-3060), so a fully-unknown node still fails loud — but a graph built only from known node *types* with WRONG or missing wiring is accepted and flattened with guessed prompt polarity. This is exactly the 'silently mis-execute' class the typed path was written to prevent, living one branch away from it.
kill_vectors.evidence: Lines 3028-3036: `if lower_title.find("negative") >= 0: _set negative else: _set prompt; saw_prompt = True`. No edge/link inspection at all in this whole block; KSampler branch (3041-3047) just copies widget fields. Contrast with the typed path which requires typed positive/negative CONDITIONING links (2665-2666).
kill_vectors.fix: Delete the 3014-3063 fallback or make it raise `[501] workflow graph body needs edges for typed execution` (the same error apply_typed_workflow_graph emits at 1607). A nodes-array with no edges should never be lowered by title heuristics; force callers through the typed/comfy-api/canvas adapters.

kill_vectors.title: Duplicated, already-diverged copy of the entire graph engine inside the daemon (dead code drift)
kill_vectors.location: serenity_daemon.mojo:268-862 (_workflow_*, _looks_like_ideogram4_comfy_ui_export, _apply_ideogram4_comfy_ui_export, _apply_workflow_params) vs the LIVE workflow_graph.mojo implementations
kill_vectors.mechanism: The daemon defines its own near-complete copy of the workflow lowering helpers and a top-level `_apply_workflow_params` (862) that is NEVER called — grep shows exactly one occurrence, the definition itself. The live path imports `apply_workflow_params as apply_workflow_graph_params` from workflow_graph.mojo (line 80) and calls THAT at parse_generate (1213). So there are two copies of safety-critical parsing (id coercion, edge validation, ideogram4 export, 501 sentinels) and they can (and already do) drift. A future fix applied to one file silently misses the other; a reader auditing the daemon sees stale logic that does not run.
kill_vectors.evidence: serenity_daemon.mojo:862 `def _apply_workflow_params(...)`; grep `\b_apply_workflow_params\b` returns only line 862 (no call site). Live call is serenity_daemon.mojo:1213 `apply_workflow_graph_params(obj)`, imported at line 80. The daemon's _workflow_id (545-555) duplicates workflow_graph.mojo:521-531.
kill_vectors.fix: Delete serenity_daemon.mojo lines ~210-862 of duplicated workflow helpers (keep only the daemon-specific _opt_int/_opt_str/_opt_num/_opt_bool used by parse_generate) and import every workflow helper from workflow_graph.mojo. One source of truth for graph lowering.

kill_vectors.title: Worklist is O(N^2) scans per pass with worst-case O(N^3) total, plus repeated full edge scans — large graphs are quadratic-to-cubic
kill_vectors.location: workflow_graph.mojo:1781-2903 (outer `while remaining > 0` x inner `for i in range(nodes_json.length())`), value lookups via _workflow_value_index (635-641) and link finders _workflow_find_input_link (553-567)
kill_vectors.mechanism: Readiness is resolved by repeated full sweeps: the outer while loop can run up to N times (a chain resolves one node per pass in the worst ordering), the inner loop is N nodes, and inside each node every input calls _workflow_find_input_link which scans ALL edges, and every resolved value calls _workflow_value_index which linearly scans the growing parallel value lists. For a deep K-input chain that is roughly O(N_passes * N_nodes * (E_edges + V_values)) — cubic in pathological orderings. There is also no upper bound on nodes/edges other than the 1 MiB request cap, so a crafted ~1 MiB graph (tens of thousands of tiny Reroute/SetNode nodes) can burn CPU and memory on the request thread, stalling the single-threaded epoll daemon for every other client (soft DoS).
kill_vectors.evidence: Outer loop 1781; inner 1783; _workflow_find_input_link scans `for i in range(edges.length())` (555) and is called multiple times per node (e.g. KSampler resolves 10 links at 2639-2648); _workflow_value_index linear scan (638). No node/edge count ceiling anywhere in apply_typed_workflow_graph; only MAX_REQUEST_BYTES=1MiB (serenity_daemon.mojo:90) bounds input.
kill_vectors.fix: Precompute once per graph: a Dict[node_id->index], a Dict[(node_id,port)->value_index], and an adjacency map (to_node -> list of edges) so link resolution and value lookup are O(1). Replace the parallel List columns with a single value-store struct. Add an explicit node/edge ceiling (e.g. 4096) that fails 501 'graph too large' before the loop. This turns the whole pass near-linear and removes the soft-DoS surface.

kill_vectors.title: Canvas adapter drops muted (mode==2) nodes only for bypass (mode==4); a muted terminal SaveImage/KSampler yields a misleading 501 instead of a clear 'output disabled' error, and muted upstream nodes leave dangling edges
kill_vectors.location: workflow_graph.mojo:1262 (comfy_ui_canvas_to_typed_graph skips only `mode == 4`) and _comfy_ui_node_index (1038, active_only filters only mode==4)
kill_vectors.mechanism: ComfyUI has TWO inactivity modes: bypass (mode 4, pass-through) and mute (mode 2, dead/no output). The canvas importer only handles mode 4. A mode-2 (muted) node is emitted as a normal typed node AND its outputs are referenced by links, but ComfyUI would NOT have produced its output — so the resulting Serenity graph executes a node the user explicitly disabled, OR if the user muted a producer feeding a still-active consumer, the consumer's link target still resolves and the muted node runs anyway. Conversely the daemon never distinguishes 'graph has no enabled output' from 'graph is malformed'. Either way the lowered result does not match what ComfyUI would do with the same canvas.
kill_vectors.evidence: workflow_graph.mojo:1262 `if _workflow_node_mode(src) == 4: continue` (no `== 2` handling); _comfy_ui_node_index:1038 `if active_only and _workflow_node_mode(node) == 4: return -1`. _workflow_node_mode (211-218) reads the raw mode int, so mode 2 is available but never acted upon.
kill_vectors.fix: Treat mode==2 (mute) as inactive in BOTH comfy_ui_canvas_to_typed_graph and _comfy_ui_node_index (skip the node and drop links touching it), matching ComfyUI semantics. If dropping a muted producer leaves a required consumer input unlinked, the existing fail-loud path will correctly raise 501 'missing required typed input'. Document bypass(4) vs mute(2) explicitly.

kill_vectors.title: Scalar INT parse and FLOAT->INT cast can overflow / wrap silently for primitive scalar nodes
kill_vectors.location: workflow_graph.mojo:1862-1875 (scalar INT bounds at full Int64 range; FLOAT branch does `int_value = Int(float_value)`)
kill_vectors.mechanism: Primitive INT scalars are parsed with bounds -9223372036854775807..9223372036854775807 (1862), i.e. effectively unbounded, then a FLOAT scalar computes `int_value = Int(float_value)` (1875) with no finite/NaN/range guard. A FloatConstant of 1e308 (allowed by the -1e308..1e308 bound at 1870) cast to Int is undefined/saturating, and that int can later be consumed where a sane bound is assumed (e.g. carried over a SetNode/GetNode bus and read as width/steps). Downstream consumers (EmptyLatentImage 1932, KSampler steps 2692) DO re-validate ranges, so this is contained to fields that take a raw scalar without a second bound — currently low impact, but the unbounded INT scalar value flows into _copy_field_if_missing for cfg/cfg-like float-from-int paths without a guard.
kill_vectors.evidence: 1862 `_workflow_int(fields, "value", 0, -9223372036854775807, 9223372036854775807)`; 1870 FLOAT bound `-1.0e308, 1.0e308`; 1875 `int_value = Int(float_value)` with no isfinite/range check.
kill_vectors.fix: Clamp scalar INT to a sane range (e.g. +/-2^31) at the source, reject non-finite FLOAT before the Int() cast, and only widen where a specific consumer needs it. Cheap defense-in-depth even though most consumers re-validate.

kill_vectors.title: ComfySwitchNode requires BOTH branches present, diverging from ComfyUI lazy-eval (unselected branch may be intentionally absent)
kill_vectors.location: workflow_graph.mojo:2118-2123 (`if not false_link.found or not true_link.found: raise 501`)
kill_vectors.mechanism: ComfyUI's SwitchNode uses check_lazy_status so only the SELECTED branch is required; the other branch can be missing/muted and the prompt still validates. This executor demands both on_false AND on_true be linked, so a valid ComfyUI graph where the user wired only the active branch (common with muted alternatives) is rejected with 501. This is a parity gap that surfaces as spurious failures, not a crash, but it contradicts the stated north-star of accepting real Comfy/Swarm exports.
kill_vectors.evidence: 2118-2123 both-required check; only after that (2132-2135) is the selected branch chosen. Compare doc note WORKFLOW_EXECUTOR_REBOOT line 186-196 which describes Comfy's lazy on_false/on_true.
kill_vectors.fix: Resolve `switch_value` first, then require ONLY the selected branch's link to be present/resolved; allow the unselected branch to be absent. This matches ComfyUI lazy semantics and the documented oracle.

comfy_comparison.how_comfy: ComfyUI execution.py builds an ExecutionList and does a real recursive/topological walk driven by which OUTPUT nodes are requested (output_nodes), executing ONLY the nodes that feed those outputs (true partial execution). It caches every node's output in a HierarchicalCache/LRUCache keyed on a recursive input hash plus IS_CHANGED, so re-running a prompt after tweaking one widget recomputes only the dirty subtree. validate_prompt runs before execution to reject bad graphs with a node-id + reason. Each node executes inside try/except that reports the failing node id, class, and full traceback to the UI. Inputs support lazy evaluation (check_lazy_status) and nodes can expand into subgraphs at runtime. nodes.interrupt_processing provides cooperative cancellation checked between nodes.
comfy_comparison.ours: Ours is a single fail-loud lowering PASS, not an output-driven executor. It always processes the WHOLE graph every call (no requested-output selection, no partial execution), holds NO output cache (apply_workflow_graph_params re-runs from raw JSON on every /v1/generate — workflow_graph.mojo:1213 call site), has no IS_CHANGED/input-hash, and resolves readiness via a repeated full-sweep worklist instead of a topo order. Validation IS up-front and fail-loud (good, matches validate_prompt intent), errors DO carry the failing input/port name and the upstream node id (e.g. 'expected CONDITIONING from 12:CONDITIONING but got LATENT', 1785-1789), but there is no traceback and the failing-NODE id is only sometimes included. Cancellation is decoupled (lowering is synchronous at enqueue; cancel_requested is checked by the worker between steps, serenity_daemon.mojo:2251), so there is no in-executor interrupt and no cancellation race — acceptable given the executor does no heavy work. We have no lazy-eval (ComfySwitchNode forces both branches) and no subgraph expansion (only two hard-coded subgraph-wrapper UUIDs).
comfy_comparison.gap: Biggest gaps vs Comfy: (1) no output-scoped partial execution — we lower every node even if it feeds nothing; (2) no output/IS_CHANGED caching — full re-parse+re-lower per request and per widget change; (3) topological correctness is achieved by brute-force fixed-point sweeps (works, but quadratic+); (4) no per-node structured error object (id+class+reason) returned to the UI — only a flat 501 string; (5) no lazy inputs / no runtime subgraph expansion.
comfy_comparison.recommendation: Adopt, in priority order: (a) a precomputed node-index + (node,port)->value map + adjacency map to replace parallel-List linear scans and the multi-sweep loop (gets you a real topo order cheaply); (b) an input-hash output cache for the lowered JobParams keyed on the canonical graph hash so unchanged graphs skip re-lowering; (c) emit a structured error {node_id, type_id, port, reason} alongside the 501 so the UI can highlight the failing node exactly like Comfy; (d) lazy-branch ComfySwitchNode to match check_lazy_status. Defer full output-driven partial execution and subgraph expansion until a real backend tensor executor exists — for a pure metadata pass the whole-graph walk is cheap once the data structures are fixed.

coordination_gaps.title: Two source-of-truth copies of the graph engine (workflow_graph.mojo live, serenity_daemon.mojo dead)
coordination_gaps.problem: serenity_daemon.mojo:268-862 re-implements the entire workflow lowering surface (id coercion, edge finders, ideogram4 export, _apply_workflow_params) but the daemon actually calls the imported workflow_graph.mojo version (line 80 import, 1213 call). The dead copy has already drifted and will keep drifting; any safety fix risks being applied to only one.
coordination_gaps.fix: Delete the duplicated helpers from serenity_daemon.mojo and import them from workflow_graph.mojo. Keep only daemon-specific _opt_* used by parse_generate.

coordination_gaps.title: Flat-params allowlist must be hand-synced with every new graph metadata field
coordination_gaps.problem: The params/genparams passthrough adapters (workflow_graph.mojo:2948-3005) carry an explicit ~70-key allowlist that is duplicated verbatim TWICE (params at 2948, genparams at 2979). Every new field the typed executor learns to emit (e.g. a future conditioning_mask_* or lanpaint_* key) must be manually added to both lists or it is silently dropped on the flat path. There is no shared constant, so the typed path and the flat path can disagree about which fields exist.
coordination_gaps.fix: Define the field list once (a single List/constant) and reuse it for both params and genparams loops; ideally derive it from the same place the executor and JobParams/IPC codec agree on, so a new field is added in one location.

coordination_gaps.title: Executor emits backend-unsupported metadata that only the BACKEND rejects, not the graph layer
coordination_gaps.problem: The typed executor cheerfully lowers inpaint/qwen-edit/lanpaint/conditioning-mask metadata into JobParams for ALL models, and correctness depends entirely on each backend remembering to call reject_unsupported_*_params(...). The parity map (COMFY_SWARM line 164) admits 'some backend incompatibilities fail after enqueue/start rather than through a uniform no-CUDA request preflight'. So a Qwen+mask graph lowers cleanly, enqueues, occupies the queue, and only fails when the worker starts — a coordination gap between graph admission and backend capability.
coordination_gaps.fix: Add a model<->capability preflight in parse_generate (after apply_workflow_graph_params) that maps the resolved model to its capability set and returns 501/422 BEFORE enqueue if the lowered params include fields the target backend cannot consume. Centralizes the reject_unsupported_* logic that is currently scattered per-backend.

coordination_gaps.title: 501 vs 422 boundary is string-prefix-based and fragile across the IR/daemon seam
coordination_gaps.problem: The entire unsupported-vs-bad-request distinction rides on every executor error string starting with the literal '[501] ' (serenity_daemon.mojo:3255 `msg.startswith("[501] ")` then byte_substr(msg,6,...)). Any new raise in the graph code that forgets the exact prefix (or uses a different spacing) silently becomes a 422 'bad request' instead of 501 'unsupported feature', misleading the UI and the parity gates. There is no enum or typed error.
coordination_gaps.fix: Introduce a typed error kind (or at minimum a single helper `unsupported(msg)` that prepends the canonical sentinel) used everywhere in workflow_graph.mojo, so the prefix cannot drift. Long-term, return a structured {status, node_id, reason} object instead of prefix-encoding the HTTP status in a string.

performance.title: Full graph re-lowering on every request, no output cache
performance.current: apply_workflow_graph_params(obj) runs the entire fixed-point worklist from raw JSON on every POST /v1/generate (serenity_daemon.mojo:1213). Tweaking one widget and resubmitting re-parses and re-lowers the whole graph; there is no IS_CHANGED/input-hash cache like ComfyUI's HierarchicalCache.
performance.suggestion: Hash the canonical workflow JSON (or the typed graph) and memoize the resulting flat JobParams JSON. On resubmit of an unchanged graph, skip lowering entirely. Even a tiny LRU keyed on the graph hash removes the dominant per-request cost for interactive widget tweaking.

performance.title: Quadratic-to-cubic worklist with linear value/edge scans
performance.current: Outer while-sweep (up to N passes) x inner per-node loop (N) x per-input full edge scan (_workflow_find_input_link, workflow_graph.mojo:553) x linear value lookup (_workflow_value_index, 635). Parallel List columns grow and are re-scanned. No node/edge ceiling under the 1 MiB cap.
performance.suggestion: Build node-index, (node,port)->value-index, and to_node->edges maps ONCE before the loop; resolve in a real topological order (Kahn) instead of multi-sweep. Replace parallel Lists with a single value-store. Add an explicit max node/edge count that 501s early. Reduces the pass to near-linear and closes the soft-DoS window on the single-threaded daemon.

performance.title: Redundant deep JSONValue copies during adapter dispatch
performance.current: apply_workflow_params copies the whole object before each adapter (wf_copy = obj.copy() at 2921/2924/2927) and the comfy-api/canvas adapters rebuild an entirely new nodes/edges JSON tree (_comfy_api_prompt_to_typed_graph 1525, comfy_ui_canvas_to_typed_graph 1253) with per-field .copy() in tight loops (e.g. 1564-1573, 1296-1305).
performance.suggestion: The detection functions (looks_like_*) already inspect obj; pass borrowed references instead of deep-copying the body before dispatch. In the typed-graph builders, move/transfer JSONValues where ownership allows instead of .copy() per edge/field. For the common 10-30 node graph this is minor, but it compounds with the no-cache re-run per request.

top_suggestions: Delete the dead duplicated graph engine in serenity_daemon.mojo (~268-862) and the legacy title-sniffing nodes-only fallback (workflow_graph.mojo:3014-3063, make it raise the same 501 as the typed path). These two are the highest-severity correctness/maintenance hazards: one is silent mis-execution, the other is guaranteed drift of safety-critical parsing.

Replace the multi-sweep parallel-List worklist with precomputed maps (node-index, (node,port)->value, to_node->edges) and a Kahn topological order; add an explicit node/edge ceiling that 501s before the loop. Removes the O(N^2)-O(N^3) blowup and the soft-DoS surface on the single-threaded epoll daemon.
Add an input-hash output cache for lowered JobParams (ComfyUI IS_CHANGED/HierarchicalCache analog) so unchanged graphs and single-widget tweaks skip re-lowering entirely.

Return a STRUCTURED error {node_id, type_id, port, reason, status} from the executor instead of prefix-encoding HTTP status in a '[501] ' string; centralize the sentinel behind one `unsupported()` helper so the 501/422 boundary cannot drift. This also lets the UI highlight the exact failing node like ComfyUI.

Move the reject_unsupported_*_params capability checks into a single model<->capability preflight in parse_generate, run BEFORE enqueue, so backend-incompatible graphs (Qwen+mask, etc.) fail 501/422 immediately instead of after the worker starts.

Honor ComfyUI mode==2 (mute) as inactive in the canvas importer (not just mode==4 bypass) and make ComfySwitchNode require only the SELECTED branch (lazy semantics). Both are real-export parity gaps that currently cause spurious 501s or graphs that don't match ComfyUI.

kill_vectors_verified.title: Legacy nodes-only adapter silently degrades unknown graphs to title-sniffed txt2img
kill_vectors_verified.location: workflow_graph.mojo:3014-3063 (reached from apply_workflow_params via wf.contains("nodes") with NO "edges" array)
kill_vectors_verified.mechanism: When a workflow body has `nodes` but no `edges` array (and is not detected as ideogram4/comfy-api/canvas/params/genparams), control falls into a second, untyped adapter. It loops nodes and for CLIPTextEncode decides positive-vs-negative purely by `title.lower().find("negative")` (3031-3035), copies KSampler/EmptyLatent fields with NO link validation, and treats VAEDecode/SaveImage as no-ops. Any node type not in its tiny if/elif DOES raise 501 (3057-3060), so a fully-unknown node still fails loud — but a graph built only from known node *types* with WRONG or missing wiring is accepted and flattened with guessed prompt polarity. This is exactly the 'silently mis-execute' class the typed path was written to prevent, living one branch away from it.
kill_vectors_verified.evidence: Lines 3028-3036: `if lower_title.find("negative") >= 0: _set negative else: _set prompt; saw_prompt = True`. No edge/link inspection at all in this whole block; KSampler branch (3041-3047) just copies widget fields. Contrast with the typed path which requires typed positive/negative CONDITIONING links (2665-2666).
kill_vectors_verified.fix: Delete the 3014-3063 fallback or make it raise `[501] workflow graph body needs edges for typed execution` (the same error apply_typed_workflow_graph emits at 1607). A nodes-array with no edges should never be lowered by title heuristics; force callers through the typed/comfy-api/canvas adapters.

verdict.claim: Legacy nodes-only adapter silently degrades unknown graphs to title-sniffed txt2img
verdict.notes: The kill-vector exists exactly as described. No guard neutralises it — the only thing that keeps a malformed graph out of this path is the presence of `"edges"` as a JSON array; omitting it or sending it as null/object is enough to reach the legacy adapter. Polarity assignment is purely title.lower().find("negative"), KSampler fields are copied with no link validation, and VAEDecode/SaveImage are explicit no-ops. The severity is "high" not "critical": the consequence is silent mis-execution (wrong prompt polarity, disconnected sampler settings applied) rather than a crash, data loss, or security breach. The claimed severity of high is accurate and not inflated.

kill_vectors_verified.title: Duplicated, already-diverged copy of the entire graph engine inside the daemon (dead code drift)
kill_vectors_verified.location: serenity_daemon.mojo:268-862 (_workflow_*, _looks_like_ideogram4_comfy_ui_export, _apply_ideogram4_comfy_ui_export, _apply_workflow_params) vs the LIVE workflow_graph.mojo implementations
kill_vectors_verified.mechanism: The daemon defines its own near-complete copy of the workflow lowering helpers and a top-level `_apply_workflow_params` (862) that is NEVER called — grep shows exactly one occurrence, the definition itself. The live path imports `apply_workflow_params as apply_workflow_graph_params` from workflow_graph.mojo (line 80) and calls THAT at parse_generate (1213). So there are two copies of safety-critical parsing (id coercion, edge validation, ideogram4 export, 501 sentinels) and they can (and already do) drift. A future fix applied to one file silently misses the other; a reader auditing the daemon sees stale logic that does not run.
kill_vectors_verified.evidence: serenity_daemon.mojo:862 `def _apply_workflow_params(...)`; grep `\b_apply_workflow_params\b` returns only line 862 (no call site). Live call is serenity_daemon.mojo:1213 `apply_workflow_graph_params(obj)`, imported at line 80. The daemon's _workflow_id (545-555) duplicates workflow_graph.mojo:521-531.
kill_vectors_verified.fix: Delete serenity_daemon.mojo lines ~210-862 of duplicated workflow helpers (keep only the daemon-specific _opt_int/_opt_str/_opt_num/_opt_bool used by parse_generate) and import every workflow helper from workflow_graph.mojo. One source of truth for graph lowering.

verdict.evidence:
serenity_daemon.mojo:862 — definition only, zero call sites:
  def _apply_workflow_params(mut obj: JSONValue) raises:  # line 862

serenity_daemon.mojo:80 + 1213 — live path:
  from serenitymojo.serve.workflow_graph import apply_workflow_params as apply_workflow_graph_params  # line 80
  apply_workflow_graph_params(obj)  # line 1213, parse_generate()

Dead copy's params key list (lines 885–891, ~20 keys including "lora"):
  "model", "prompt", "prompt_raw", "negative", "width", "height",
  "steps", "seed", "cfg", ..., "mask_image", "creativity", "lora"

Live workflow_graph.mojo apply_workflow_params (line 2948–2969, ~40+ keys):

  "reference_latent_method", "qwen_edit_conditioning_image",
  "lanpaint_mask_channel", "lanpaint_lambda", ... (20+ additional fields)

Dead copy no-workflow dispatch handles only ideogram4 (lines 871–874).
Live version handles three variants: ideogram4 + comfy_api_prompt_graph + comfy_ui_canvas_graph (lines 2919–2928).

grep -rn "_apply_workflow_params" serenitymojo/ → exactly one hit: line 862 (the definition).
verdict.claim: serenity_daemon.mojo contains a dead, already-diverged copy of the entire workflow lowering subsystem (~594 lines, functions 268–862) that is never called; the live path imports and calls apply_workflow_params from workflow_graph.mojo instead.
verdict.notes: The claim is structurally correct and the drift is real: the dead copy's params key lists are ~20 keys shorter than the live version, and the dead copy is missing two ComfyUI graph dispatch branches that the live version handles. However, severity should be medium, not high. No user request currently reaches the dead code — all entry points go through the correctly-imported live function. The risk is future-maintainer confusion (reading stale logic and thinking it runs) or accidentally wiring a call to the wrong function. There is no active safety-critical misparsing today. Recommend deleting lines 268–862 from serenity_daemon.mojo and relying solely on workflow_graph.mojo imports.

reviews.section: Graph->Backend Dispatch & Routing (dispatch_backend.mojo, backend.mojo, model_scan.mojo, serenity_daemon.mojo routing/mode-select)
reviews.summary: The dispatch layer is structurally sound for the fail-loud "constrained adapter" goal: unknown models raise at start() (no silent txt2img fallback), the JSON parser caps nesting at MAX_DEPTH=512 and request bodies at 1 MiB, and the typed graph executor 501s on unknown nodes. But routing is split across THREE independent model->kind classifiers (sampler_backend_for_model in sampler_registry, _kind_for_model in dispatch_backend, _isolated_kind in process_isolated_backend) that disagree: SD3/Qwen are "runnable" to the parse path but raise at backend start, so they are admitted with HTTP 200 and only fail AFTER enqueue as a failed job — the exact "fail after start, not at preflight" gap the parity map calls out. The DispatchBackend in-process VRAM reclaim is a documented measured no-op (pool trim reclaims 0 MiB), so a zimage->qwen switch OOMs in-process; only the "isolated" mode (kill child) actually reclaims. Routing is duplicated/inconsistent and several substring matches are dangerously broad (e.g. "l2p", "anima" inside "animagine"). No request-rate or graph-node-count bound exists. Comfy-style per-node error reporting, output caching, and partial execution are entirely absent (acceptable for the current flat-flatten model, but a real gap vs ComfyUI).

kill_vectors.title: Disabled/unknown-at-backend models are admitted with HTTP 200, then fail post-enqueue instead of at preflight
kill_vectors.location: serenitymojo/serve/serenity_daemon.mojo:1222-1223 + 3246-3293; serenitymojo/sampling/sampler_registry.mojo:119-126; serenitymojo/serve/dispatch_backend.mojo:58-122
kill_vectors.mechanism: parse_generate() classifies the model with sampler_backend_for_model() purely to pick defaults. That function returns valid-looking keys ('sd3','disabled','qwenimage') for models that _kind_for_model() (the dispatch admission classifier) RAISES on (sd3/qwen/flux/ltx/wan via _known_disabled_model_reason at dispatch_backend.mojo:58-82, and qwenimage has KIND_QWEN defined at :52 but _kind_for_model NEVER returns it). So a POST with model='stable-diffusion-3-large' or 'qwen-image-2512' passes parse_generate, returns HTTP 200 with a job_id, fans out into JobRecords (3270-3287), and only fails later inside tick_worker->backend.start() (2291-2302) as state='failed'. The user gets a 200 + a silently-failing job rather than an immediate 501/422. The parity map (COMFY_SWARM...md 'Exact Current Blockers', last bullet) explicitly flags this as a known gap.
kill_vectors.evidence: parse_generate: `p.model = _opt_str(...); var sampler_backend = sampler_backend_for_model(p.model, default_model)` (1222-1223) is used ONLY for defaults (1227-1248, 1345); there is no `if sampler_backend == 'disabled': raise`. sampler_registry.mojo:119 `if m.find('sd3')... return 'sd3'`, :125 qwen->'qwenimage'. dispatch_backend.mojo:62 `if lo.find('sd3')... ` inside _known_disabled_model_reason -> :102 `raise Error(...)`. tick_worker:2290-2302 catches the start() raise into jobs[i].state='failed'.
kill_vectors.fix: Add a single shared admission classifier (e.g. classify_model(model) -> (kind, runnable, reason)) used by BOTH parse_generate (raise '[501] '+reason at HTTP time) and the backends. At minimum, in parse_generate after line 1223, call _kind_for_model(p.model) inside a try and convert its raise to a 501 before enqueue, so disabled/unknown models never reach the queue.

kill_vectors.title: Three divergent model->kind routing tables can silently route a request to the wrong backend on add/edit
kill_vectors.location: serenitymojo/sampling/sampler_registry.mojo:89-131; serenitymojo/serve/dispatch_backend.mojo:85-122; serenitymojo/serve/process_isolated_backend.mojo:100-109
kill_vectors.mechanism: sampler_backend_for_model (used to pick defaults), _kind_for_model (DispatchBackend admission), and _isolated_kind (which reuses _kind_for_model) each re-implement substring matching with DIFFERENT order and DIFFERENT token sets. Example divergence: sampler_registry routes 'klein'/'flux2'/'flux-2' -> 'flux2' and picks scheduler 'flux2' (registry:160-161), but _kind_for_model routes 'klein' -> KIND_KLEIN while a bare 'flux2'/'flux-2' name (no 'klein') falls through every branch in _kind_for_model (107-114) to the final `raise` at :115. So a model literally named 'flux2_something' gets flux2 sampler defaults from parse but is REJECTED at start — different code paths disagree on whether the same string is runnable. Order also matters: 'sdxl' is matched before 'anima' in both, but the disabled-reason scan runs before klein/sdxl in _kind_for_model only, not in sampler_backend_for_model, so the two tables can classify the same name into incompatible buckets as new model families are added.
kill_vectors.evidence: sampler_registry.mojo:123 `if m.find('klein')>=0 or m.find('flux2')>=0 or m.find('flux-2')>=0: return 'flux2'`. dispatch_backend.mojo:107 `if lo.find('klein')>=0: return KIND_KLEIN` (no flux2 branch) -> :115 `raise Error("unknown model '"+model+...)`. process_isolated_backend.mojo:109 `return _kind_name(_kind_for_model(model))` reuses the dispatch table, so isolated and in-process agree with each other but BOTH disagree with the parse-time defaults table.
kill_vectors.fix: Collapse all three into one authoritative routing module returning a struct {kind, sampler_backend, runnable, reason}. parse_generate, DispatchBackend, and ProcessIsolatedBackend must all call it. A grep-gate script (one already exists: scripts/check_allowed_daemon_cli_backends.py per git status) should assert the tables can't drift.

kill_vectors.title: In-process DispatchBackend model switch OOMs (pool trim is a measured 0-MiB no-op) — silent for the user, hard for the box
kill_vectors.location: serenitymojo/serve/dispatch_backend.mojo:209-246 (_free_current), 248-281 (_ensure_kind), 284-298 (start)
kill_vectors.mechanism: On a model switch in 'dispatch' mode, _free_current() drops the resident backend List and calls cu_mempool_trim_current(0) to return VRAM to the OS. The code's own comment (227-237) documents this is MEASURED to reclaim 0 MiB on this Mojo runtime because the runtime allocates from its own pool, not the CUDA default pool. So switching zimage(~21GB peak)->qwen leaves zimage's bytes pinned and the new model's first forward OOMs. The OOM surfaces as a start()/step() failure (failed job), and on a shared desktop a CUDA OOM during a large alloc can also destabilize the session. The only correct path is 'isolated' mode, but 'dispatch' is still a user-selectable mode (main:3694, 3698-3700) with no guard preventing a real multi-model switch.
kill_vectors.evidence: dispatch_backend.mojo:231-237 comment: 'it RECLAIMS 0 MiB because the runtime does NOT allocate from that default pool ... so the bytes stay pinned and switching zimage(~21GB peak) -> qwen OOMs in qwen's 1024² forward. ... The real fix is process isolation per resident model'. _ensure_kind:251-281 constructs the new backend with no free-success check.
kill_vectors.fix: Either make 'dispatch' mode refuse a cross-kind switch (raise a clear '[501] in-process model switching is unsupported on this runtime; use isolated mode' in _ensure_kind when self.kind != KIND_NONE and kind differs) or alias 'dispatch' to the isolated process-per-model path. Do not let an in-process switch reach a CUDA alloc that is known to OOM.

kill_vectors.title: Model/LoRA names from disk are interpolated into shell commands in model_scan; a crafted filename can inject
kill_vectors.location: serenitymojo/serve/model_scan.mojo:63-72 (_shell), 102-110 (_list_safetensors), 243-245 (_dir_size), 254-255 (_dir_exists)
kill_vectors.mechanism: _list_safetensors builds `find -L '<dir>' ... | sort > SCAN_TMP` and _dir_size builds `du -sb '<dir>'` then runs them through libc system(). dir is a comptime constant so those are safe, BUT the same single-quote-wrapping pattern is the project's only quoting and there is no escaping of embedded single quotes. Today dir values are constants, so this is latent rather than live; however filenames discovered by find are later passed as ScanEntry.path/name into JobParams.loras and into klein/sample_cli backends which DO shell out with those names (sample_cli_backend.mojo:_shell_quote is used there, so those are quoted). The model_scan path itself doesn't quote-escape, so if any scanned directory ever becomes attacker/user-controlled (e.g. a future per-user models dir), a name containing a single quote breaks out of the quoting.
kill_vectors.evidence: model_scan.mojo:63-72 _shell copies cmd verbatim into external_call['system']. :107-110 `String("find -L '") + dir + "' -maxdepth 1..."` — dir concatenated with no escape of embedded quotes. Contrast sample_cli_backend.mojo:75-85 _shell_quote which DOES escape `'` -> `'\''`.
kill_vectors.fix: Route every model_scan shell call through a shared _shell_quote (the one in sample_cli_backend) and/or replace the find/du/test shellouts with the existing libc FFI (sys_open/file_size already imported) plus a readdir FFI, eliminating system() from the scan path entirely.

kill_vectors.title: Overbroad substring routing tokens cause mis-disable / mis-route of legitimate models
kill_vectors.location: serenitymojo/serve/dispatch_backend.mojo:66 ('l2p'), 109 ('anima' vs 'animagine'); serenitymojo/sampling/sampler_registry.mojo:106 ('l2p'), 115-117
kill_vectors.mechanism: `lo.find('l2p') >= 0` (dispatch:66 and registry:106) disables ANY model whose name contains the 3 chars 'l2p' anywhere — a future checkpoint named e.g. 'model_v1_l2png' or a hash containing 'l2p' is silently routed to 'disabled' and rejected. Separately, sdxl is matched by `lo.find('sdxl') or 'animagine'` at dispatch:109 BEFORE anima at :112, and registry:115 matches 'animagine'->sdxl while :117 matches 'anima'->anima; 'animagine' correctly hits sdxl first, but any sdxl model whose name also contains 'anima' as a substring (it does: anim-a-gine) relies entirely on branch ORDER, which differs subtly between the two tables and is fragile to reordering. These are classification correctness bugs, not crashes, but they SILENTLY route to the wrong/disabled backend — the worst failure class for a 'fail-loud' design.
kill_vectors.evidence: dispatch_backend.mojo:66 `if ... or lo.find('l2p') >= 0: return String('Z-Image L2P execution is disabled...')`. :109 `if lo.find('sdxl')>=0 or lo.find('stable-diffusion-xl')>=0 or lo.find('animagine')>=0: return KIND_SDXL` then :112 anima. sampler_registry.mojo:115-118 same tokens, but the disabled-token scan is inline (92-112) and ordered differently than dispatch's _known_disabled_model_reason.
kill_vectors.fix: Anchor tokens to word/separator boundaries (match 'l2p' only as a suffix/segment like '_l2p'/'-l2p'/'.l2p' or the exact known names listed in the scanner) and unify the single routing table so branch order is defined once. Add a unit fixture of every known checkpoint name asserting its routed kind.

kill_vectors.title: images=N fanout uses unbounded seed arithmetic check but no per-job graph re-validation; a model that switches mid-fanout reloads N times
kill_vectors.location: serenitymojo/serve/serenity_daemon.mojo:3270-3287; serenitymojo/serve/dispatch_backend.mojo:251-281
kill_vectors.mechanism: A single /v1/generate with images=N (capped 1..64 at 1274) enqueues N JobRecords that all carry the SAME model. They run serially. This is fine for one model, but because DispatchBackend frees+reconstructs on every kind change and the daemon is serial, interleaving two different-model requests in the queue causes a free/reload on each boundary. There's no batching and no 'same model, skip reload' fast-path verification at the dispatch layer beyond `if self.kind == kind: return` (251) — which is correct, but the N fanout children each re-enter start() and each calls _kind_for_model again (re-parsing the model string N times). Not a crash; wasted work + the OOM risk above is multiplied if the queue alternates models.
kill_vectors.evidence: 3271 `var child = p.copy()` inside `for image_i in range(requested_images)`; each child later hits tick_worker:2291 backend.start(child.params) which re-runs _kind_for_model (dispatch_backend.mojo:285). 1274 `p.images = _opt_int(obj,'images',1,1,64)`.
kill_vectors.fix: Cache the resolved kind on the parsed JobParams once and pass it through (the IPC already ships every field; add 'kind'); skip re-classification per fanout child. Keep the serial reload but log a warning when the queue forces a cross-kind switch so the OOM risk is visible.

comfy_comparison.how_comfy: ComfyUI validates the whole prompt up-front (execution.validate_prompt) BEFORE any node runs, returning per-node errors to the UI; it executes a topological ExecutionList computing only the nodes feeding requested outputs (partial execution); it caches node outputs in an LRUCache/HierarchicalCache keyed on a recursive input hash + IsChangedCache, so changing one widget re-runs only the dirty subtree; each node runs in its own try/except that reports the failing node id + traceback to the client; cancellation is cooperative via nodes.interrupt_processing checked between nodes; and node selection is by an explicit class registry (NODE_CLASS_MAPPINGS), never substring matching on a free-text model field.
comfy_comparison.ours: We have no up-front whole-request validation that maps to backend runnability (parse_generate validates schema/types only; backend admission is deferred to start()). We flatten the graph to a single flat JobParams and run ONE backend serially — no per-node execution, no partial execution, no output cache, no IsChangedCache. Errors are reported as a whole-job 'failed' state with a single error string, not per-node. Cancellation is the cooperative cancel()/step() loop (backend.mojo contract, tick_worker:2251-2253) which is good. Backend selection is substring matching across 3 divergent tables (kill vector 2) rather than an explicit registry.
comfy_comparison.gap: The three highest-value Comfy ideas we lack: (1) validate_prompt-style admission — classify+reject the model/feature at HTTP time so 200-then-fail can't happen; (2) an explicit node/model registry replacing substring routing; (3) per-node/per-stage error attribution so the UI knows WHICH part failed. Output caching and partial execution matter less today because we flatten to one backend call, but they will matter the moment the executor runs real graph nodes.
comfy_comparison.recommendation: Adopt Comfy's validate-before-run contract NOW by adding a single classify_model()+feature-support preflight in parse_generate that converts backend-unrunnable models to a 501 before enqueue (fixes kill vectors 1 & 2 together). Replace the substring routing tables with one registry keyed on the model_scan arch tag (already computed) plus an alias map, mirroring NODE_CLASS_MAPPINGS. Defer output-cache/partial-exec until the executor runs real per-node tensor ops.

coordination_gaps.title: arch-tag namespace (model_scan) is disjoint from routing-token namespace (dispatch/registry)
coordination_gaps.problem: model_scan.detect_arch emits tags like 'flux-2/klein','zimage','qwen-image','sdxl' (model_scan.mojo:160-184), but _kind_for_model and sampler_backend_for_model route on the raw MODEL NAME STRING, not the arch tag. So the scanner's careful header-probe arch detection is thrown away at dispatch time and re-derived (worse) from the name substring. A checkpoint whose filename doesn't contain 'klein' but whose header IS klein (arch 'flux-2/klein') will be mis-routed or rejected even though the scanner knew its family.
coordination_gaps.fix: Route on the model_scan arch tag. /v1/models already returns arch per entry; have the UI send (or the daemon resolve) the arch and dispatch on it, so header truth wins over filename guessing.

coordination_gaps.title: LoRA compatibility uses exact arch-string equality that can never match Klein
coordination_gaps.problem: _model_lora_compatible requires m == t (serenity_daemon.mojo:2724). A Klein model's arch is 'flux-2/klein' (model_scan.mojo:164) but detect_lora_target_arch returns 'flux' for Flux-family LoRAs (model_scan.mojo:230-233) and has NO 'flux-2/klein' branch. So every real Klein LoRA is reported as incompatible with every Klein model in /v1/models, even though klein_backend DOES load AI-Toolkit/Comfy Flux2-Klein LoRAs (per the reboot doc). The UI compatibility card contradicts what the backend actually accepts.
coordination_gaps.fix: Add a family-equivalence map (klein/flux-2 LoRAs <-> 'flux-2/klein' models) instead of string equality, and add explicit klein LoRA markers to detect_lora_target_arch. Make the UI compatibility verdict consistent with backend admission, which is the documented source of truth (model_scan.mojo:30).

coordination_gaps.title: IPC start contract ships every JobParams field but worker re-runs backend.start validation with no schema-version check
coordination_gaps.problem: encode_start/decode_start (ipc_codec.mojo) hand-serialize ~60 fields by name with no protocol-version guard, while backend.mojo declares BACKEND_PROTOCOL_VERSION=1 that is never sent on the wire. If the parent and child binaries ever differ (e.g. a rebuilt parent, stale child during a rolling change), a missing field silently decodes to its JobParams default (decode_start uses `if obj.contains(...)` guards, :120-212) — e.g. a dropped 'mask_image' would silently become txt2img instead of failing loud, violating the project's core 'never silently degrade inpaint to txt2img' rule.
coordination_gaps.fix: Send BACKEND_PROTOCOL_VERSION in encode_start and reject a mismatched/absent version in decode_start (fail loud). For safety-critical fields (mask_image, inpaint_*, reference_image), require presence rather than defaulting.

performance.title: Model string re-classified on every job and every fanout child
performance.current: _kind_for_model / sampler_backend_for_model run a dozen substring scans per call; called at parse time AND per fanout child AND again at dispatch start() (serenity_daemon.mojo:1223, 3271-3287; dispatch_backend.mojo:285).
performance.suggestion: Classify once in parse_generate, store the resolved kind/sampler_backend on JobParams, ship it over IPC, and have DispatchBackend/worker trust it (re-validating only the kind enum, not re-substring-matching the name).

performance.title: /v1/models rescans disk (find/du shellouts + full header reads) on every request
performance.current: scan_checkpoints/scan_loras (model_scan.mojo:258-311) shell out to find+du and pread+parse every safetensors header on each GET /v1/models (serenity_daemon.mojo:2926-2927). No caching, blocking system() calls on the HTTP path.
performance.suggestion: Cache the scan keyed on directory mtime; invalidate only when the checkpoints/loras dirs change. Replace find/du/test system() calls with the libc FFI already imported (file_size/sys_open) to avoid fork+exec on the hot path.

performance.title: In-process pool trim runs two ctx.synchronize() per job boundary for 0 reclaim
performance.current: between_jobs_trim and _free_current each call ctx.synchronize() twice around cu_mempool_trim_current(0), which is documented to reclaim 0 MiB on this runtime (dispatch_backend.mojo:333-349, 226-242).
performance.suggestion: In dispatch mode, drop the trim+double-sync (it's a measured no-op that only adds latency); rely on isolated mode for real reclaim. Keep one synchronize() if needed for ordering.

top_suggestions: P0 — Unify routing into ONE classify_model(model)->{kind,sampler_backend,runnable,reason} module consumed by parse_generate, DispatchBackend (_kind_for_model), and ProcessIsolatedBackend (_isolated_kind). Eliminates the 3-table drift (kill vectors 1,2) and the SD3/Qwen 200-then-fail asymmetry.

P0 — Add admission-time preflight in parse_generate: after resolving the model, call the classifier and raise '[501] '+reason for disabled/unknown models so they return 501 at HTTP time instead of enqueuing a job that fails later (serenity_daemon.mojo ~1223). This is the Comfy validate_prompt analogue.

P1 — Make in-process 'dispatch' mode refuse a known-OOM cross-kind switch (raise in dispatch_backend.mojo:_ensure_kind when switching kinds) or alias 'dispatch' to the isolated process-per-model path; never let a switch reach the CUDA alloc the code documents as OOMing.

P1 — Fix the LoRA-compatibility contract: replace exact arch-string equality (_model_lora_compatible, serenity_daemon.mojo:2724) with a family-equivalence map and add klein/flux-2 LoRA markers to detect_lora_target_arch so Klein LoRAs aren't reported incompatible with Klein models. Route dispatch on the model_scan header arch tag, not the filename substring.

P2 — Send BACKEND_PROTOCOL_VERSION over the worker IPC (ipc_codec.encode_start/decode_start) and require presence of safety-critical fields (mask_image, inpaint_*, reference_image) so a parent/child mismatch fails loud instead of silently defaulting inpaint to txt2img.

P2 — Anchor routing substrings to separator boundaries ('l2p'->'_l2p' etc.) and add a name->expected-kind fixture; route model_scan shell calls through _shell_quote (or libc FFI) to close the latent filename-injection surface and cache /v1/models scans by dir mtime.

kill_vectors_verified.title: Disabled/unknown-at-backend models are admitted with HTTP 200, then fail post-enqueue instead of at preflight
kill_vectors_verified.mechanism: parse_generate() classifies the model with sampler_backend_for_model() purely to pick defaults. That function returns valid-looking keys ('sd3','disabled','qwenimage') for models that _kind_for_model() (the dispatch admission classifier) RAISES on (sd3/qwen/flux/ltx/wan via _known_disabled_model_reason at dispatch_backend.mojo:58-82, and qwenimage has KIND_QWEN defined at :52 but _kind_for_model NEVER returns it). So a POST with model='stable-diffusion-3-large' or 'qwen-image-2512' passes parse_generate, returns HTTP 200 with a job_id, fans out into JobRecords (3270-3287), and only fails later inside tick_worker->backend.start() (2291-2302) as state='failed'. The user gets a 200 + a silently-failing job rather than an immediate 501/422. The parity map (COMFY_SWARM...md 'Exact Current Blockers', last bullet) explicitly flags this as a known gap.
kill_vectors_verified.evidence: parse_generate: `p.model = _opt_str(...); var sampler_backend = sampler_backend_for_model(p.model, default_model)` (1222-1223) is used ONLY for defaults (1227-1248, 1345); there is no `if sampler_backend == 'disabled': raise`. sampler_registry.mojo:119 `if m.find('sd3')... return 'sd3'`, :125 qwen->'qwenimage'. dispatch_backend.mojo:62 `if lo.find('sd3')... ` inside _known_disabled_model_reason -> :102 `raise Error(...)`. tick_worker:2290-2302 catches the start() raise into jobs[i].state='failed'.
kill_vectors_verified.fix: Add a single shared admission classifier (e.g. classify_model(model) -> (kind, runnable, reason)) used by BOTH parse_generate (raise '[501] '+reason at HTTP time) and the backends. At minimum, in parse_generate after line 1223, call _kind_for_model(p.model) inside a try and convert its raise to a 501 before enqueue, so disabled/unknown models never reach the queue.

verdict.claim: Disabled/unknown-at-backend models are admitted with HTTP 200, then fail post-enqueue instead of at preflight
verdict.notes: The kill-path is exactly as described. _kind_for_model (dispatch_backend.mojo:85, called only at dispatch_backend.mojo:285 inside start()) is never reachable during request parsing. parse_generate uses sampler_backend_for_model from sampler_registry, which silently returns "sd3"/"qwenimage" for disabled models. The POST handler sees no exception and returns 200 + job_id. The failure surfaces only when tick_worker calls backend.start(), which calls _kind_for_model, which raises via _known_disabled_model_reason; that raise is caught by the tick_worker try/except and stored as jobs[i].state="failed". One nuance vs the claim: qwen models do NOT fail via a missing-KIND path — they fail at _known_disabled_model_reason (dispatch_backend:60-61) before any KIND_QWEN branch is reachable, making KIND_QWEN effectively dead code. The severity is correctly high: callers get a successful 200 response with a job_id that will never produce output. The fix is a preflight call to _kind_for_model (or an equivalent disabled-model check) inside parse_generate, converting the deferred raise into an immediate 422/501.

kill_vectors_verified.title: Three divergent model->kind routing tables can silently route a request to the wrong backend on add/edit

Example divergence: sampler_registry routes 'klein'/'flux2'/'flux-2' -> 'flux2' and picks scheduler 'flux2' (registry:160-161), but _kind_for_model routes 'klein' -> KIND_KLEIN while a bare 'flux2'/'flux-2' name (no 'klein') falls through every branch in _kind_for_model (107-114) to the final `raise` at :115.
